pattern: Two lists of different lengths reach a shared tail at different step counts, but rerouting each pointer through the other list once it runs out equalizes the remaining distance so they meet exactly at the join.
hints: Comparing values as you walk both lists together only works if the lengths already match — they usually don't.
Send each pointer down its own list and, on reaching the end, redirect it to the head of the OTHER list.
After at most one redirect each, both pointers have covered the same total distance and now move in lockstep to the join, or both hit null together if there is none.

edge_inputs.description: intersection in the middle of both chains
the two heads are the same node — full overlap
intersection at the very last node
single-node A with no intersection — B must not be mistaken for touching it
single-node A that IS the intersection point
B's unique prefix is much longer than all of A, testing the pointer-swap alignment
longer six-node A with intersection roughly halfway

stress.description: random A chain with a random disjoint B prefix, intersecting or not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    a_vals = rng.sample(range(1, 1000000), n)
    intersect = rng.random() < 0.6
    attach = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if intersect else None
    m = rng.randint(0, min(size, 300))
    b_vals = list(rng.sample(range(1000000, 2000000), m)) if m else []
    return [a_vals, {"values": b_vals, "attach": attach}]
